message:
feat(conformance): expand benchmark workloads and add batched linalg differential tests

Benchmark suite (benchmark.rs, run_performance_budget_gate.rs, baseline JSON):
- Add 7 new benchmark workloads: large broadcast add (1024x1024 by 1024),
  matmul (256x256 by 256x256), quicksort (1M elements), FFT (65536),
  astype f64->i32 (1024x1024), reshape (1024x1024 to 2048x512), and
  npy save+load round-trip (512x512 f64).
- Register corresponding performance budget entries with p95 ceilings for
  each new workload, promoting reshape/view, dtype-conversion, and
  npy-parse paths from the uninstrumented list to full budget tracking.
- Regenerate ufunc_benchmark_baseline.json with all 10 workloads captured.

Batched linalg operations (lib.rs, linalg_differential_cases.json):
- Wire up batch_det, batch_trace, batch_inv, and batch_solve operations
  in the conformance differential executor via execute_linalg_operation.
- Add flatten_matrix_fixture helper to convert fixture row-major matrices
  into flat f64 slices expected by the batch linalg API surface.
- Add tolerance-aware validation path for batch operation results in
  validate_linalg_differential_expectation.
- Add 4 two-lane 2x2 fixture cases (identity + non-trivial) covering
  batch_det, batch_trace, batch_inv, and batch_solve.

Cargo.toml:
- Add criterion 0.5.1 dev-dependency and criterion_core_ops bench target
  to support criterion-based micro-benchmarking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,3 +19,10 @@ serde = { version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0.149"
 serde_yaml = "0.9.34"
 sha2 = "0.10.9"
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+criterion = "0.5.1"
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "criterion_core_ops"
+harness = false

@@ -74,6 +74,11 @@ const WORKLOAD_BUDGETS: &[WorkloadBudget] = &[
         path_family: "broadcast add/mul",
         p95_budget_ms: 180.0,
     },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "ufunc_add_broadcast_1024x1024_by_1024",
+        path_family: "broadcast add/mul",
+        p95_budget_ms: 1300.0,
+    },
     WorkloadBudget {
         name: "reduce_sum_axis1_keepdims_false_256x256",
         path_family: "reduction sum/mean",
@@ -84,14 +89,42 @@ const WORKLOAD_BUDGETS: &[WorkloadBudget] = &[
         path_family: "reduction sum/mean",
         p95_budget_ms: 210.0,
     },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "matmul_256x256_by_256x256",
+        path_family: "matmul/dot",
+        p95_budget_ms: 2400.0,
+    },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "sort_quicksort_1m",
+        path_family: "sorting/searching",
+        p95_budget_ms: 1600.0,
+    },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "fft_65536",
+        path_family: "fft transforms",
+        p95_budget_ms: 1200.0,
+    },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "astype_f64_to_i32_1024x1024",
+        path_family: "dtype conversion",
+        p95_budget_ms: 950.0,
+    },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "reshape_1024x1024_to_2048x512",
+        path_family: "reshape/view operations",
+        p95_budget_ms: 250.0,
+    },
+    WorkloadBudget {
+        name: "io_npy_save_load_512x512_f64",
+        path_family: "npy parse + load",
+        p95_budget_ms: 1800.0,
+    },
 ];
 
 const UNINSTRUMENTED_BUDGET_PATHS: &[&str] = &[
-    "reshape/view operations",
-    "dtype conversion",
-    "npy parse + load",
     "memory footprint",
     "allocation churn",
+    "allocator fragmentation under adversarial workloads",
 ];
 
 fn main() {
